seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in LlamaIndex Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in LlamaIndex agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In LlamaIndex, Context Window Management for AI Agents manifests as challenges in balancing retrieved node contexts within token limits while ensuring relevance and completeness for agent reasoning. Agents using query engines often face token overflow from excessive retrieved nodes, irrelevant chunks polluting the context, or chunking artifacts splitting critical information across boundaries. This leads to degraded agent performance, hallucinations, and incomplete task execution in RAG pipelines.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent receives 15+ irrelevant nodes exceeding 128k token window
howItManifests.symptom: Query engine returns 'context too long' error or truncated response
howItManifests.impact: Agent fails to answer simple queries, high latency, token waste

howItManifests.scenario: Synthesizer processes mixed relevant/irrelevant chunks in agent context
howItManifests.symptom: Agent confidently cites wrong documents or fabricates details
howItManifests.impact: Hallucinations appear factual, erodes user trust in agent outputs

howItManifests.scenario: Vector index not refreshed after document updates
howItManifests.symptom: Agent returns stale pricing/product info from old embeddings
howItManifests.impact: Critical business decisions based on outdated context

howItManifests.scenario: Sentence splitting chunks code block across node boundaries
howItManifests.symptom: Agent misinterprets partial functions/classes, generates broken code
howItManifests.impact: Developer agents produce non-compilable code suggestions

detection.name: Token Overflow Monitor
detection.description: Detects when retrieved nodes exceed 80% of context window before query execution
detection.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader

from llama_index.core.retrievers import VectorIndexRetriever


docs = SimpleDirectoryReader('data/').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(docs)

retriever = VectorIndexRetriever(index=index, similarity_top_k=10)

# Detection
nodes = retriever.retrieve('What is the latest pricing?')
total_tokens = sum(node.get_content().count(' ') for node in nodes)

if total_tokens > 32000:  # 80% of 40k window
    print(f'⚠️ TOKEN OVERFLOW: {total_tokens} tokens from {len(nodes)} nodes')
    print(f'Query engine will truncate or fail')
else:
    print(f'✅ Context OK: {total_tokens} tokens')

detection.name: Relevance Score Drift
detection.description: Flags when average node similarity drops below threshold indicating irrelevant retrievals
detection.code:
from llama_index.core.query_engine import RetrieverQueryEngine
from llama_index.core.postprocessor import SimilarityPostprocessor

query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    similarity_top_k=5,
    node_postprocessors=[SimilarityPostprocessor(similarity_cutoff=0.7)]
)

# Detection
response = query_engine.query('2024 pricing updates')
node_scores = [node.score for node in response.source_nodes]

avg_relevance = sum(node_scores) / len(node_scores)
if avg_relevance < 0.75:
    print(f'⚠️ LOW RELEVANCE: avg score {avg_relevance:.2f}')
    print('Agent will likely hallucinate from poor context')

detection.name: Index Freshness Check
detection.description: Detects documents older than threshold in retrieved context
detection.code:
import datetime
from llama_index.core.schema import TextNode

# Detection
def check_index_staleness(nodes, max_age_days=7):
    today = datetime.datetime.now()
    stale_count = 0
    
    for node in nodes:
        if hasattr(node.metadata, 'last_modified'):
            doc_date = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(node.metadata['last_modified'])
            if (today - doc_date).days > max_age_days:
                stale_count += 1
                print(f'Stale node: {node.metadata.get("filename", "unknown")}')
    
    if stale_count > 0:
        print(f'⚠️ STALE CONTEXT: {stale_count}/{len(nodes)} nodes outdated')
    return stale_count

# Usage
nodes = retriever.retrieve('current pricing')
check_index_staleness(nodes)

fixes.name: Context Window Compressor
fixes.description: Automatically compresses retrieved context to fit token limits using LlamaIndex compressor
fixes.code:
from llama_index.core.indices.postprocessor import MetadataReplacementPostProcessor
from llama_index.core.postprocessor import LLMMetadataExtractor, SimilarityPostprocessor
from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI

llm = OpenAI(model="gpt-4")
metadata_extractor = LLMMetadataExtractor(
    llm=llm, 
    prompt_template="Extract the relevant info about {context_str} for answering {query_str}"
)

query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    similarity_top_k=8,
    node_postprocessors=[
        SimilarityPostprocessor(similarity_cutoff=0.76),
        metadata_extractor,
        MetadataReplacementPostProcessor(target_metadata_key="node_info")
    ]
)

response = query_engine.query('What are current prices?')

fixes.name: Chunk Overlap Fixer
fixes.description: Uses semantic chunking with overlap to prevent boundary errors
fixes.code:
from llama_index.core.node_parser import SentenceSplitter
from llama_index.embeddings.openai import OpenAIEmbedding

splitter = SentenceSplitter(
    chunk_size=512,
    chunk_overlap=100,
    paragraph_separator="\n\n",
    secondary_chunking_regex=r'[^\.!?\n]+[\.!?]+\s*'
)

# Rebuild with better chunking
service_context = ServiceContext.from_defaults(
    embed_model=OpenAIEmbedding(),
    llm=OpenAI(model="gpt-4")
)

index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(
    docs,
    service_context=service_context,
    transformations=[splitter]
)

fixes.name: Auto-Refreshing Index
fixes.description: Implements automatic index refresh on document changes

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically monitors LlamaIndex query engines for context window violations, irrelevant retrievals, and index staleness during agent execution. By wrapping `query_engine.query()` with `vex.verify()`, Vex captures real-time metrics like token usage, node relevance scores, and response faithfulness, flagging issues before they impact agent performance.

Vex provides automated remediation through dynamic context truncation, relevance filtering, and index refresh triggers. When context overflow is detected, Vex can automatically apply compressors or reduce `similarity_top_k`. For stale indexes, Vex triggers rebuilds. The platform maintains audit trails of all context decisions, enabling teams to optimize retrieval pipelines systematically.

Integration takes <5 minutes and works across all LlamaIndex retrievers, routers, and agent workflows. Vex's observability dashboard shows context health trends, helping teams proactively tune chunk sizes, embedding models, and refresh cadences.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader

# Install: pip install llama-index vex-sdk

# Initialize your LlamaIndex agent

docs = SimpleDirectoryReader('data/').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(docs)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine(similarity_top_k=6)

# Vex monitors context window, relevance, freshness
@vex.verify(
    checks=[
        'context_window_overflow',
        'retrieval_relevance_lt_0.75',
        'index_staleness_gt_7d',
        'response_faithfulness_lt_0.9'
    ],
    auto_fix=True  # Applies compressors, filters automatically
)
def agent_query(question: str):
    """Vex-monitored agent query"""
    response = query_engine.query(question)
    return str(response)

# Usage
result = agent_query('What are the 2024 product prices?')
print(result)

# Vex dashboard: http://localhost:8080
vex.start_dashboard()

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add `vex.verify()` to your LlamaIndex agents in 2 minutes and eliminate context window failures forever.
